course_description.overview: The Granfondo route starts and finishes in Seia, passing through Manteigas to the summit finish at Torre (1,993m), Portugal's highest point in mainland, entirely within Serra da Estrela Natural Park, featuring relentless climbs through iconic high-mountain landscapes like the Zêzere Glacier Valley.[1][2][3]

key_segments.name: Seia to Manteigas
key_segments.description: Initial rolling terrain building into mountain approaches.

key_segments.name: Zêzere Glacier Valley ascent
key_segments.description: Grueling, scenic U-shaped valley climb toward Torre summit.

key_segments.name: Torre Summit Finish
key_segments.distance: 30 km time trial or final push
key_segments.description: Iconic ascent to 1,993m, the queen stage highlight.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Known as Portugal's Marmotte, this is the toughest amateur cycling event in the country with 4,600m elevation gain over 174 km, equivalent to climbing the Eiffel Tower four times, demanding high endurance and climbing ability.[1][3]

logistics.getting_there: Fly into Lisbon or Porto airports, then drive 3-4 hours to Seia/Manteigas in Serra da Estrela region; local shuttles may be available.[2]
logistics.accommodation: Book in Seia or Manteigas; event headquarters in Manteigas with post-race activities in Seia; options near Serra da Estrela Natural Park.[2][3]
logistics.registration_details: Online via official site; early bird ~50-55 EUR until May 31, 2026; 50% refund if withdrawn by May 31; closes June 21; age 19+ for Granfondo.[1][2]
logistics.bike_logistics: Standard road bike; transport to Seia recommended; check official for bike check-in.[1]
logistics.local_considerations: High altitude (up to 1,993m), potential fog and variable June weather; within natural park, respect environment; cultural ties like Bread Museum in Seia.[3]

final_verdict.one_liner: Portugal's toughest granfondo: epic climbs to Torre with stunning Serra da Estrela views.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Strong climbers and endurance riders seeking a Marmotte-style challenge in high mountains.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the prestige, scenery, and value as Europe's hidden gem granfondo.
pro_tips: Train for sustained 4,600m+ climbing; acclimate to altitude.
Register early for discounts; book Seia/Manteigas stays.
Opt for Granfondo for full Torre experience; watch weather at summit.
Fuel well at 7 aid stations; respect 17:00 cutoff.
